title: Clearing the air
desc: Resolve a misunderstanding with a friend
setting: Roberto has been short with you all day. You sense something's wrong and decide to address it.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Dijiste que venías ayer y nunca llegaste.
steps.en: You said you were coming yesterday and never showed up.

options.es: Creo que hubo un malentendido. Yo entendí hoy.
options.en: I think there was a misunderstanding. I understood today.
options.feedback: Naming it as a misunderstanding (not blaming) opens the door to fixing it calmly.
Flat denial escalates things. Even if you're sure, lead with curiosity, not defense.
Honest if true — but if it was really a mix-up, say so rather than take false blame.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: ¿En serio? Yo te dije bien claro que era ayer.
steps.en: Really? I told you clearly it was yesterday.

options.es: Puede que no te entendí bien. Disculpá.
options.en: Maybe I didn't understand you well. Sorry.
options.feedback: Taking partial responsibility cools things without grovelling. Very mature.
Turning it into blame reignites the conflict. The goal is to repair, not win.
Brushing it off can feel dismissive when he's clearly bothered. Acknowledge his feeling.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Bueno, tal vez los dos nos confundimos.
steps.en: Well, maybe we both got confused.

options.en: Surely. The important thing is to clear it up.
options.feedback: Refocusing on resolution over fault — exactly how to close a misunderstanding.

options.es: Sí, no quiero que quedemos mal por esto.
options.en: Yes, I don't want us to be on bad terms over this.
options.feedback: Naming that you value the friendship is warm and disarming. Beautiful.
Dismissive — he just offered an olive branch. Meet it, don't swat it away.

options.en: All good. Thanks for talking it out.
options.feedback: Appreciating the conversation itself strengthens the bond. Perfect close.

options.en: Done. Next time I'll confirm clearly.
options.feedback: Owning a small fix going forward shows real maturity. Nicely done.
A jab at the end undoes the repair. End warm when someone offers peace.
